visa_types.documents_required:
Travel insurance is mandatory for all Schengen countries. If you have not purchased, Please click here (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/are/en/grc/travel-insurance)
Up to date personal bank account statement for the last 3 months original and stamped from the bank
Click here (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/5csevOMmZxCBLomY9V13cK/943211f09f34c445a69ae22c73bbfa2b/short-stay-checklist-2023.pdf) to download the checklist
visa_types.visa_fees:
[Table:   Medical Treatment]
Please note:
- Please be informed that the fees charged by the Embassy or VFS Global are based on the current exchange rate in local currency and may be subject to change without prior notification.
- Each applicant shall be charged a service charge of AED 88 (all applicable taxes included) per application in addition to the visa fees.
- All fees are non-refundable.
 Visa Waiver:
-  The visa fee shall be waived for applicants belonging to one of the following categories:
- Family members of Greek/EU citizens;
- Holders of diplomatic and service passports;
- school pupils, students, postgraduate students and accompanying teachers who undertake stays for the purpose of study or educational training;
- researchers from third countries travelling for the purpose of carrying out scientific research as defined in Recommendation No 2005/761/EC of the European Parliament and
                                                                                    of the Council of 28 September 2005 to facilitate the issue by the Member States of uniform short-stay visas for researchers from third countries travelling within the
                                                                                    Community for the purpose of carrying out scientific research;
- representatives of non-profit organisations aged 25 years or less participating in seminars, conferences, sports, cultural or educational events organised by non-profit organisations
visa_types.processing_time:
The processing time for applications is minimum 15 working days, as per the Schengen Regulation, In exceptional cases, this period may be extended up to 30 calendar days, notably when
                                                                        further scrutiny of the application is needed and when additional documentation is needed in specific cases, the period may be extended up to a maximum of 60 calendar days.
Please be advised that due to the above mentioned minimum processing time, we are not able to guarantee the return of passports by specific dates for travel.

visa_types.overview:
Overview
This visa applies to those looking to receive medical treatment in Greece 
Incomplete, unclear or unsigned applications will not be accepted.
If the visit involves two or more Schengen countries, the Embassy responsible for the issuing the visa will be: 
- The Embassy of the country in which the traveler is going to stay the longest.
- The Embassy of the country of first entry if the traveler is to stay the same amount of time in different Schengen countries.
It is advisable to plan your travel well in advance to avoid any inconvenience
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
This visa applies to those looking to receive medical treatment in Greece 
Incomplete, unclear or unsigned applications will not be accepted.
If the visit involves two or more Schengen countries, the Embassy responsible for the issuing the visa will be: 
- The Embassy of the country in which the traveler is going to stay the longest.
- The Embassy of the country of first entry if the traveler is to stay the same amount of time in different Schengen countries.
It is advisable to plan your travel well in advance to avoid any inconvenience

## Visa Fees

[Table:   Medical Treatment]
Please note:
- Please be informed that the fees charged by the Embassy or VFS Global are based on the current exchange rate in local currency and may be subject to change without prior notification.
- Each applicant shall be charged a service charge of AED 88 (all applicable taxes included) per application in addition to the visa fees.
- All fees are non-refundable.
 Visa Waiver:
-  The visa fee shall be waived for applicants belonging to one of the following categories:
- Family members of Greek/EU citizens;
- Holders of diplomatic and service passports;
- school pupils, students, postgraduate students and accompanying teachers who undertake stays for the purpose of study or educational training;
- researchers from third countries travelling for the purpose of carrying out scientific research as defined in Recommendation No 2005/761/EC of the European Parliament and
                                                                                    of the Council of 28 September 2005 to facilitate the issue by the Member States of uniform short-stay visas for researchers from third countries travelling within the
                                                                                    Community for the purpose of carrying out scientific research;
- representatives of non-profit organisations aged 25 years or less participating in seminars, conferences, sports, cultural or educational events organised by non-profit organisations

## Documents Required
Travel insurance is mandatory for all Schengen countries. If you have not purchased, Please click here (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/are/en/grc/travel-insurance)
Up to date personal bank account statement for the last 3 months original and stamped from the bank
Click here (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/5csevOMmZxCBLomY9V13cK/943211f09f34c445a69ae22c73bbfa2b/short-stay-checklist-2023.pdf) to download the checklist 

## Photo Specifications
Applicants are requested to submit their application form with the new photo format, as explained below: 
General
The photo must fulfill the norm specified by the ICAO (International Civil Aviation Organization).
- Use plain white background.
- The photo must be no more than 6-months old.
- Portrait-style picture ( no slopping or posing), showing both edges of face clearly.
- Close up of the head and top of the shoulders so that the face takes up 70–80% of the photograph.
- Photo in sharp focus and clear.
Photo Size
- The picture dimensions must be at least 240 pixels wide by 320 pixels high at least. A smaller picture will be rejected. For instance, a photo being 235 pixels by 320 pixels is not
                                                                            valid.
- The photo should keep a proportion of 3 x 4 (width x height) within limits. (i.e. 250 x 340 pixels picture would be valid, but not a 290 x 340 pixels one).
- The image quality (size in Kbytes) can never be bigger than 120 Kbytes.
Resolution
- An image resolution is measured in DPI (dots per inch) and it's mainly used to control printing quality. Therefore, higher resolution means better printing quality.
- Photos resolution should be 300 dpi. When you scan the image, check this parameter carefully. However, bear in mind that the higher the resolution, the bigger the picture.
- The only image format accepted is JPEG (Join Photograph Expert Group Format). The other image formats such as BMP, TIFF, GIF, PNG, etc. will be rejected.
Attaching the Photo to the Application form
- Glue one photograph to the application form in the designated space.
Please follow these instructions carefully. If the photograph provided does not meet these requirements, your application will be considered incomplete.

## Processing Time
The processing time for applications is minimum 15 working days, as per the Schengen Regulation, In exceptional cases, this period may be extended up to 30 calendar days, notably when
                                                                        further scrutiny of the application is needed and when additional documentation is needed in specific cases, the period may be extended up to a maximum of 60 calendar days.
Please be advised that due to the above mentioned minimum processing time, we are not able to guarantee the return of passports by specific dates for travel. 

## Download Forms
Please fill the form online completing all details of the applicant and submit it along with the required documents at the Visa Application Centre.
Schengen visa application form (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/1f7izdwoollYpr4oFFoTi9/82a19a24463bef6e383d79e07ecffade/greece-application-form.pdf)- PDF

visa_types.overview:
Overview
A tourist visa allows you to stay in the Schengen area for a short period 
Incomplete, unclear or unsigned applications will not be accepted.
If the visit involves two or more Schengen countries, the Embassy responsible for issuing the visa will be: 
- The Embassy of the country in which the traveler is going to stay the longest.
- The Embassy of the country of first entry, if the traveler is to stay in different Schengen countries for the same amount of time.
It is advisable to plan your travel well in advance to avoid any inconvenience
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
A tourist visa allows you to stay in the Schengen area for a short period 
Incomplete, unclear or unsigned applications will not be accepted.
If the visit involves two or more Schengen countries, the Embassy responsible for issuing the visa will be: 
- The Embassy of the country in which the traveler is going to stay the longest.
- The Embassy of the country of first entry, if the traveler is to stay in different Schengen countries for the same amount of time.
It is advisable to plan your travel well in advance to avoid any inconvenience

## Visa Fees

[Table:   Tourism - Visiting Family and Friends]
Please note:
- Please be informed that the fees charged by the Embassy or VFS Global are based on the current exchange rate in local currency and may be subject to change without prior notification.
- Each applicant shall be charged a service charge of AED 88 (all applicable taxes included) per application in addition to the visa fees.
- All fees are non-refundable.

- The visa fee shall be waived for applicants belonging to one of the following categories:
- Family members of Greek/EU citizens;
- Holders of diplomatic and service passports;
- school pupils, students, postgraduate students and accompanying teachers who undertake stays for the purpose of study or educational training;

                                                                                    Community for the purpose of carrying out scientific research;
- representatives of non-profit organisations aged 25 years or less participating in seminars, conferences, sports, cultural or educational events organised by non-profit organisations;

## Documents Required

## Photo Specifications
Applicants are requested to submit their application form with the new photo format, as explained below: 
General
The photo must fulfill the norm specified by the ICAO (International Civil Aviation Organization).
- Use plain white background.
- The photo must be no more than 6-months old.
- Portrait-style picture ( no slopping or posing), showing both edges of face clearly.
- Close up of the head and top of the shoulders so that the face takes up 70–80% of the photograph.

Photo Size
- The picture dimensions must be at least 240 pixels wide by 320 pixels high at least. A smaller picture will be rejected. For instance, a photo being 235 pixels by 320 pixels is not
                                                                            valid.
- The photo should keep a proportion of 3 x 4 (width x height) within limits. (i.e. 250 x 340 pixels picture would be valid, but not a 290 x 340 pixels one).
- The image quality (size in Kbytes) can never be bigger than 120 Kbytes.
Resolution
- An image resolution is measured in DPI (dots per inch) and it's mainly used to control printing quality. Therefore, higher resolution means better printing quality.
- Photos resolution should be 300 dpi. When you scan the image, check this parameter carefully. However, bear in mind that the higher the resolution, the bigger the picture.
- The only image format accepted is JPEG (Join Photograph Expert Group Format). The other image formats such as BMP, TIFF, GIF, PNG, etc. will be rejected.
Attaching the Photo to the Application form
- Glue one photograph to the application form in the designated space.
Please follow these instructions carefully. If the photograph provided does not meet these requirements, your application will be considered incomplete.

## Processing Time
The processing time for applications is minimum 15 working days, as per the Schengen Regulation, In exceptional cases, this period may be extended up to 30 calendar days, notably when
                                                                        further scrutiny of the application is needed and when additional documentation is needed in specific cases, the period may be extended up to a maximum of 60 calendar days.
Please be advised that due to the above mentioned minimum processing time, we are not able to guarantee the return of passports by specific dates for travel. 

## Download Forms
Please fill the form online completing all details of the applicant and submit it along with the required documents at the Visa Application Centre.

visa_types.overview:
Overview
This visa applies to those who are officially invited to visit Greece as members of a delegation.
Incomplete, unclear or unsigned applications will not be accepted.
If the visit involves two or more Schengen countries, the Embassy responsible for issuing the visa will be:
- The Embassy of the country in which the traveler is going to stay the longest.
- The Embassy of the country of first entry if the traveler is to stay the same time in different Schengen countries.
It is advisable to plan your travel well in advance to avoid any inconvenience.

- Holders of diplomatic and service passports;
- school pupils, students, postgraduate students and accompanying teachers who undertake stays for the purpose of study or educational training;
- researchers from third countries travelling for the purpose of carrying out scientific research as defined in Recommendation No 2005/761/EC of the European Parliament and
                                                                                    of the Council of 28 September 2005 to facilitate the issue by the Member States of uniform short-stay visas for researchers from third countries travelling within the
                                                                                    Community for the purpose of carrying out scientific research;
- representatives of non-profit organisations aged 25 years or less participating in seminars, conferences, sports, cultural or educational events organised by non-profit organisations

                                                                        further scrutiny of the application is needed and when additional documentation is needed in specific cases, the period may be extended up to a maximum of 60 calendar days.
                                                                    
Please be advised that due to the above mentioned minimum processing time, we are not able to guarantee the return of passports by specific dates for travel.

visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
Airport Transit visa allows you to wait for a connecting flight in the international area of the airport. This visa does not grant access to the Schengen area.
Incomplete, unclear or unsigned applications will not be accepted.
If the visit involves two or more Schengen countries, the Embassy responsible for issuing the visa will be: 
- The Embassy of the country in which the traveler is going to stay the longest.
- The Embassy of the country of first entry if.the traveler is to stay the same amount of time in different Schengen countries
It is advisable to plan your travel well in advance to avoid any inconvenience

## Visa Fees

[Table:   Transit]
Please note:
- Please be informed that the fees charged by the Embassy or VFS Global are based on the current exchange rate in local currency and may be subject to change without prior notification.
- Each applicant shall be charged a service charge of AED 88 (all applicable taxes included) per application in addition to the visa fees.
- All fees are non-refundable.
 Visa Waiver:
-  The visa fee shall be waived for applicants belonging to one of the following categories:
- Family members of Greek/EU citizens;
- Holders of diplomatic and service passports;
- school pupils, students, postgraduate students and accompanying teachers who undertake stays for the purpose of study or educational training;
- researchers from third countries travelling for the purpose of carrying out scientific research as defined in Recommendation No 2005/761/EC of the European Parliament and
                                                                                    of the Council of 28 September 2005 to facilitate the issue by the Member States of uniform short-stay visas for researchers from third countries travelling within the
                                                                                    Community for the purpose of carrying out scientific research;
- representatives of non-profit organisations aged 25 years or less participating in seminars, conferences, sports, cultural or educational events organised by non-profit organisations

## Documents Required
Travel insurance is mandatory for all Schengen countries. If you have not purchased, Please click here (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/are/en/grc/travel-insurance)
Click here (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/5csevOMmZxCBLomY9V13cK/943211f09f34c445a69ae22c73bbfa2b/short-stay-checklist-2023.pdf) to download the checklist 

## Photo Specifications
Applicants are requested to submit their application form with the new photo format, as explained below: 
General
The photo must fulfill the norm specified by the ICAO (International Civil Aviation Organization).
- Use plain white background.
- The photo must be no more than 6-months old.
- Portrait-style picture ( no slopping or posing), showing both edges of face clearly.
- Close up of the head and top of the shoulders so that the face takes up 70–80% of the photograph.
- Photo in sharp focus and clear.
Photo Size
- The picture dimensions must be at least 240 pixels wide by 320 pixels high at least. A smaller picture will be rejected. For instance, a photo being 235 pixels by 320 pixels is not
                                                                            valid.
- The photo should keep a proportion of 3 x 4 (width x height) within limits. (i.e. 250 x 340 pixels picture would be valid, but not a 290 x 340 pixels one).
- The image quality (size in Kbytes) can never be bigger than 120 Kbytes.
Resolution
- An image resolution is measured in DPI (dots per inch) and it's mainly used to control printing quality. Therefore, higher resolution means better printing quality.
- Photos resolution should be 300 dpi. When you scan the image, check this parameter carefully. However, bear in mind that the higher the resolution, the bigger the picture.
- The only image format accepted is JPEG (Join Photograph Expert Group Format). The other image formats such as BMP, TIFF, GIF, PNG, etc. will be rejected.
Attaching the Photo to the Application form
- Glue one photograph to the application form in the designated space.
Note: If children are traveling with their parents and are included inside the passport of the mother and/or the father, a separate application has to be submitted for
                                                                        each child. 
Please follow these instructions carefully. If the photograph provided does not meet these requirements, your application will be considered incomplete.

## Processing Time
The processing time for applications is minimum 15 working days, as per the Schengen Regulation, In exceptional cases, this period may be extended up to 30 calendar days, notably when
                                                                        further scrutiny of the application is needed and when additional documentation is needed in specific cases, the period may be extended up to a maximum of 60 calendar days.
                                                                    
Please be advised that due to the above mentioned minimum processing time, we are not able to guarantee the return of passports by specific dates for travel. 

## Download Forms
Please fill the form online completing all details of the applicant and submit it along with the required documents at the Visa Application Centre.
Schengen visa application form (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/1f7izdwoollYpr4oFFoTi9/82a19a24463bef6e383d79e07ecffade/greece-application-form.pdf)- PDF

visa_types.name: Long Stay National Visa
visa_types.overview:
A national “D” visa allows you to enter Greece in order to apply for a residence permit and the application for the same is to be be submitted directly at the Hellenic Embassy in Abu
                                                            Dhabi. This visa can be issued under various categories (i.e. work, studies, training, etc. ) and has a long-stay validity for a maximum of one year. Holders of a national visa are obligated
                                                            to apply for a residence permit to Greece immediately after their first entry to the country.
Please click here (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/48IQ0Rj4suSdIQU9yP35HH/054b5901f95c6212695936a780dfaa3d/National-Visa-Application.pdf) to download the application form for National “D” visa.
visa_types.full_text:
A national “D” visa allows you to enter Greece in order to apply for a residence permit and the application for the same is to be be submitted directly at the Hellenic Embassy in Abu
                                                            Dhabi. This visa can be issued under various categories (i.e. work, studies, training, etc. ) and has a long-stay validity for a maximum of one year. Holders of a national visa are obligated
                                                            to apply for a residence permit to Greece immediately after their first entry to the country.
Please click here (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/48IQ0Rj4suSdIQU9yP35HH/054b5901f95c6212695936a780dfaa3d/National-Visa-Application.pdf) to download the application form for National “D” visa.

- The only image format accepted is JPEG (Join Photograph Expert Group Format). The other image formats such as BMP, TIFF, GIF, PNG, etc. will be rejected.
Attaching the Photo to the Application form
- Glue one photograph to the application form in the designated space.
Please follow these instructions carefully. If the photograph provided does not meet these requirements, your application will be considered incomplete.

## Processing Time
 The processing time for applications is minimum 15 working days, as per the Schengen Regulation, In exceptional cases, this period may be extended up to 30 calendar days, notably when
                                                                        further scrutiny of the application is needed and when additional documentation is needed in specific cases, the period may be extended up to a maximum of 60 calendar days.
                                                                    
Please be advised that due to the above mentioned minimum processing time, we are not able to guarantee the return of passports by specific dates for travel. 

## Download Forms
Please fill the form online completing all details of the applicant and submit it along with the required documents at the Visa Application Centre.
Schengen visa application form (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/1f7izdwoollYpr4oFFoTi9/82a19a24463bef6e383d79e07ecffade/greece-application-form.pdf)- PDF

visa_types.name: Short Stay Visa
visa_types.overview:
- Before applying for a visa, please be sure about your purpose of visit
- Foreign nationals who wish to travel to Greece must be in possession of a valid residence/work visa of the country they are applying from, unless they are nationals from countries which are not
                                                                included in the list of countries with the obligation to carry a visa in order to cross external borders for stays in the Schengen Area. The holder of a Schengen visa is entitled to enter
                                                                and stay, for a maximum of 90 days in any- 180 days period, from the date of entry into the Schengen Area.
- Schengen visa has to be obtained from the Embassy/Consulate of the country where the main purpose of travel lies. Therefore, please check your travel plans before making your application to the
                                                                Embassy of Greece. In case you are travelling to multiple Schengen countries, please submit your application to the Embassy/Consulate where you will be staying for maximum number of days.
                                                                If the duration of stay is the same for all Schengen countries you are visiting, please submit your application to the Embassy/Consulate of point of your first entry.
- A Schengen visa allows you to travel to the following countries: 

[Table:Short Stay Visa]
Please note: VFS Global staff can provide you with information and application forms for your application, but they are not permitted to advise you regarding the visa category you
                                                            should choose. Our work is primarily administrative and non-judgmental in nature. The final assessment on your visa, and the duration of visa processing, is entirely at the prerogative of The
                                                            Embassy of Hellenic Republic (Greece) in Abu Dhabi. Please note that the Embassy of Greece in Abu Dhabi issues visas only to residents of - UAE.
An application for a Schengen visa can be lodged at the Embassy of Greece in the following cases: 
- Greece is the sole destination of the applicant
- If the applicant intends to travel to more Schengen countries: Greece is the main destination of the applicant or, where there is no main destination, Greece is his/her first point of entry in
                                                                the Schengen area.
Processing time :
The processing time for applications is maximum 15 days, as per the Schengen Regulation, In exceptional cases, this period may be extended up to 30 calendar days, notably when further scrutiny of the application is needed and when additional documentation is needed in specific cases, the period may be extended up to a maximum of 60 calendar days.
It is advisable to lodge the application as early as possible and at least 15 calendar days before the intended visit. Please note that applications can be lodged no more than six months before the start of the intended visit.
Visa Fee Waivers
(Art. 16 Visa Code; VFA’s visa exemptions; Directive 2004/38)
- Applicants shall pay a visa fee of EUR 90.
- Nationals from Armenia, Azerbaijan, Cape Verde, Georgia and Ukraine shall pay a visa fee of EUR 35. This also applies to citizens of Albania, Bosnia-Herzegovina, the Former Yugoslav Republic of Macedonia (FYROM), the Republic of Moldova, Montenegro and Serbia NOT holding biometric passports.

- The visa fee shall be waived for applicants belonging to one of the following categories:
- Children 0- 12 years old.
- Family members of Greek /EU citizens.
- Holders of diplomatic and service passports.
- School pupils, students, postgraduate students and accompanying teachers who undertake stays for the purpose of study or educational training.
- Researchers from third countries travelling for the purpose of carrying out scientific research as defined in Recommendation No 2005/761/EC of the European Parliament and of the Council of 28 September 2005 to facilitate the issue by the Member States of uniform short-stay visas for researchers from third countries travelling within the Community for the purpose  of carrying out scientific research; representatives of non-profit organisations aged 25 years or less participating in seminars, conferences, sports, cultural or educational events  organised by non-profit organisations.
- Schengen visa must be obtained from the Embassy/Consulate of the country where the main purpose of travel lies. Therefore, please check your travel plans before submitting your application. In case of travel to multiple Schengen countries, please make your application to the one where you will be staying for the maximum number of days. If the duration of stay is the same in more than one Schengen country during the first trip, you must submit your visa application at the Embassy/Consulate of your first point of entry.
- Please book appointments well in advance depending on your intended date of travel to avoid any inconvenience. Applications cannot be submitted earlier than six (6) months before the travel date. Please refer to the processing time specific to this Mission.
